architecture.summary: Small transformer models trained to solve maze tasks. The paper analyzes internal representations in the residual stream and token embeddings, showing that maze topology and valid paths are encoded in structured form. Linear decoding from a single token's residual stream reconstructs the entire maze. Attention heads termed 'adjacency heads' are identified as implicated in selecting valid next tokens during path-following.
trained_components: transformer model trained on maze-solving task

biblio.title: Structured World Representations in Maze-Solving Transformers

claims.statement: the residual stream of only a single token can be linearly decoded to faithfully reconstruct the entire maze
the learned embeddings of individual tokens have spatial structure
attention heads (dubbed adjacency heads) are implicated in finding valid subsequent tokens
evidence for the consistent emergence of structured internal representations of maze topology and valid paths
